texto: Culture has lead us to betray our own aboriginal spirit and wholeness, into an ever-worsening realm of synthetic, isolating, impoverishing estrangement. Which is not to say that there are no more everyday pleasures, without which we would loose our humanness. But as our plight deepens, we glimpse how much must be erased for our redemption.
explicacao: Orientação direta de John Zerzan sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “Culture has lead us to betray our own aboriginal spirit and wholeness, into an ever-worsening rea…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, culture, which, útil em momentos de reflexao.
